methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: xAI

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.6B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
Crusoe Energy is a Series E AI & Software company founded by Chase Lochmiller and Cully Cavness, currently valued at $10B+ with $1.375B total capital raised. The peer set comprises 9 comparables—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms—with a focus on 5 same-stage AI & Software peers: xAI, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, WEKA, and Modal, valued between $1.1B and $250.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, grounded in the $10.0B last post-money valuation, applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement, implying $14.0B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $3.8B, though the peer set spans $1.1B to $250.0B, reflecting significant variance in the AI sector. The wide range reflects outsized valuations in frontier AI (xAI at $250.0B) versus infrastructure-focused peers clustering below $6B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3.2B to $16.1B, with a median of $8.9B. The current $10B+ valuation sits above peer median but below stage-progression upside. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—driven by xAI's extreme outlier status—and the applicability of stage-progression assumptions in a volatile frontier-tech environment where funding multiples vary sharply by subsector and investor appetite.
